data.title: code-reviewer-standard: when staged diff is narrow, reviewer generates critical findings for files outside the diff -> unnecessary resolution loop entered
data.description:
## Incident Overview

During epic 0e62-ff06 (worktree-20260413-104622), the standard-tier reviewer was dispatched against a 2-file diff (plugins/dso/skills/debug-everything/SKILL.md + tests/skills/test-debug-everything-gha-scanner.sh). It returned a correctness score of 1 and four critical findings, all referencing files not in the staged diff:

- plugins/dso/hooks/lib/pre-edit-write-functions.sh
- plugins/dso/skills/preplanning/tests/test_adversarial_review_prompts.sh
- plugins/dso/scripts/check-referential-integrity.sh
- plugins/dso/scripts/dso-setup.sh

All four findings were factually wrong (hallucinated bugs about BASH_SOURCE path depth and quote types). Verification against the actual code confirmed each was a false positive.

## Actual Behavior

The reviewer scanned beyond the staged diff and fabricated critical bugs in unrelated files. record-review.sh accepted the review (file-overlap check did not reject it) and recorded status: failed. The orchestrator then entered the Autonomous Resolution Loop, dispatching a resolution sub-agent to apply REVIEW-DEFENSE comments to four unrelated files, re-staging them, re-computing the diff hash, and running two additional re-reviews.

## Expected Behavior

Option A: record-review.sh should reject reviewer output when all finding file fields fall outside the staged diff (no overlap), surfacing an error before the resolution loop is entered. The existing file-overlap rejection path (ERROR: reviewer findings files do not overlap with any changed files in the diff) should apply here but did not.

Option B: The reviewer itself should not generate findings for files it was not asked to review. Findings should be scoped to files present in the diff provided.

## Impact

- ~6 additional sub-agent dispatches (resolution + two re-reviews)
- Four unrelated files gained REVIEW-DEFENSE comments that exist solely to satisfy a false positive
- Total wasted cost: significant tokens and wall-clock time

## Reproduction

Dispatch dso:code-reviewer-standard against a narrow diff (2-3 files). Observe whether findings reference files outside the diff. The hallucination appears more likely when the diff is small and the reviewer lacks sufficient signal from the diff alone.

## Investigation Starting Points

1. Check record-review.sh file-overlap logic — why did it not reject the out-of-scope findings?
2. Review the reviewer prompt for scope-limiting instructions — does it explicitly tell the reviewer to restrict findings to staged files only?
3. Consider whether a pre-filter step should strip finding entries whose file field is not in the staged diff before passing to record-review.sh.
